question: Is the thin silver ring clearly visible on the index finger, consistent with the subject reference?
Is the ink stain on the thumb clearly preserved from the subject reference?
Are the fingernail shapes (short, natural curvature) preserved from the subject reference?
Is the background completely replaced with a deep blue base color to mimic an engineering blueprint?
Are the hand, notebook, and pencil rendered using cyan-white lines instead of photorealistic shading?
Is there a visible hierarchy of line weights (e.g., thicker outlines vs. thinner detail lines)?
Are the dimension arrows and dashed lines from the structure reference present in the generated image?
Does the hand pose strictly match the structure reference (fingers curled, grip configuration)?
Does the pencil angle and orientation strictly follow the structure reference?
Is the spiral binding of the notebook positioned on the left side, matching the structure reference?
Is the image free of readable text annotations, containing only lines and symbols?
Are the lines rendered cleanly and crisply, without being shaky, wobbly, or blurry?
Is the hand anatomically correct, with five fingers and proper joint proportions (no malformed or missing digits)?
Is the image free from visual artifacts like ghosting, smeared edges, or inconsistent blending between elements?
